get.summary: GET /api/active-sessions — returns all active session files from ~/.claude/sessions/.
GET /api/branches - Get distinct list of branch names across all sessions.
get.description:
Returns a sorted array of unique branch names found in the database.
Excludes sessions without a branch (NULL git_branch).

200.description: Sorted list of unique branch names

get.summary: GET /api/check-path?path=... — Check whether a filesystem path still exists.
get.description:
Used by the frontend to validate project paths before offering "resume session".
Worktree directories can be removed, making the session un-resumable.

post.summary: POST /api/classify — Trigger a classification job.

200.description: Classification job started or dry-run result

post.summary: POST /api/classify/cancel — Cancel a running classification job.
POST /api/classify/single/:session_id — Classify a single session synchronously.
post.description:
Bypasses ClassifyState entirely — no job record, no SSE.
Returns the classification result directly.
Uses dedicated O(1) DB queries — NOT the bulk session list.

get.summary: GET /api/classify/status — Get classification status.
GET /api/classify/stream — SSE stream of classification progress.

200.description: SSE stream of classification progress events

get.summary: GET /api/claude-code-settings — returns merged, redacted Claude Code settings.

post.summary: POST /api/cli-sessions -- Create a new tmux-backed CLI session.
post.description:
Blocks until the Claude process inside tmux writes its pid.json, then
returns the real Claude session UUID. The Born handler (sessions_lifecycle)
creates the LiveSession and sets tmux ownership naturally — no intermediate
"Spawning" entry needed.

503.description: tmux unavailable or Claude failed to start

delete.description:
`id` is the tmux session name (e.g. "cv-abc123"). Finds the corresponding
LiveSession by tmux ownership and reaps it.

get.summary: GET /api/coaching/rules -- List all coaching rules from the rules directory.

200.description: All coaching rules from the rules directory

post.summary: POST /api/coaching/rules -- Create a new coaching rule file.

delete.summary: DELETE /api/coaching/rules/{id} -- Remove a coaching rule file.

400.description: Rule not found or invalid ID

get.summary: GET /api/contributions - Main contributions page data.

200.description: Contribution metrics for the specified period

get.summary: GET /api/export/sessions - Export all sessions.
get.description:
Query parameters:
- format: "json" (default) or "csv"

JSON format returns structured data per A5.2 spec.
CSV format returns RFC 4180 compliant CSV with proper escaping.

get.summary:
GET /api/facets/badges?ids=id1,id2,id3 — Quality badges (outcome +
satisfaction) for the requested session IDs. Returns a JSON map keyed
by session ID.

200.description: Session quality badges keyed by session ID

get.summary:
GET /api/facets/ingest/stream — SSE stream polling FacetIngestState atomics
every 200ms. Emits "progress" events repeatedly, then a terminal "done"
event when the ingest reaches Complete, Error, or NoCacheFound.

200.description: SSE stream of facet ingest progress events

post.summary:
POST /api/facets/ingest/trigger — Start facet ingest from the Claude Code
insights cache. Returns immediately with `{"status": "started"}` or
`{"status": "already_running"}` if an ingest is already in progress.

200.description: Facet ingest triggered or already running

get.summary:
GET /api/facets/pattern-alert — Check the most recent sessions for a
negative satisfaction pattern. Returns `{pattern, count, tip}` if a
pattern is detected, or `{pattern: null}` otherwise.
GET /api/facets/stats — Aggregate statistics across all session facets.
GET /api/health - Health check endpoint.
get.description: Returns server status, version, and uptime.

get.summary: `GET /api/ide/detect` — return cached list of installed IDEs.

post.summary: `POST /api/ide/open` — open a file in the requested IDE.

get.summary: SSE handler that streams indexing progress events.
get.description:
# Events

| Event name       | When emitted                              |
|------------------|-------------------------------------------|
| `status`         | Indexer enters `ReadingIndexes` phase      |
| `ready`          | Projects/sessions discovered, UI can load  |
| `deep-progress`  | Each batch of deep-indexed sessions        |
| `done`           | Indexing complete                          |
| `error`          | Indexing failed                            |

The stream terminates after `done` or `error`.

200.description: SSE stream of indexing progress events

get.summary: GET /api/indexing/status -- lightweight JSON snapshot of indexing progress.
get.description:
Returns the current phase, indexed count, and total count.
Designed for polling (every 200-300 ms) from the frontend during rebuilds.

get.summary: GET /api/insights - Compute and return behavioral insights.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max patterns to return. Defaults to 50.

200.description: Behavioral insights with patterns and classification status

get.summary: GET /api/insights/benchmarks - Compute personal progress benchmarks.

parameters.name: range
parameters.description: Time range: all, 30d, 90d, 1y. Defaults to all.

200.description: Personal progress benchmarks vs past periods

get.summary: GET /api/insights/categories - Returns hierarchical category data.

GET /api/insights/models — per-model usage aggregated from rollup
tables.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description:
Period start (unix seconds). Pair with `to`; omit both for
all-time.

parameters.name: bucket
parameters.description:
Rollup bucket granularity. Valid: `daily`, `weekly`, `monthly`.
Defaults to `daily`.

Smaller buckets scan more rows; for ranges >= 180 days prefer
`weekly` or `monthly` to keep row count bounded. The aggregation
sum is identical across granularities.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
Max rows to return after descending-by-total-tokens sort.
Defaults to 100. Hard-capped at 500 server-side to bound payload
size.

200.description: Per-model aggregated usage for the requested range

get.summary:
GET /api/insights/projects — per-project usage aggregated from
rollup tables.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description:
Period start (unix seconds). Pair with `to`; omit both for
all-time.

parameters.name: bucket
parameters.description:
Rollup bucket granularity. Valid: `daily`, `weekly`, `monthly`.
Defaults to `daily`.

Smaller buckets scan more rows; for ranges >= 180 days prefer
`weekly` or `monthly` to keep row count bounded. The aggregation
sum is identical across granularities.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
Max rows to return after descending-by-total-tokens sort.
Defaults to 100. Hard-capped at 500 server-side to bound payload
size.

200.description: Per-project aggregated usage for the requested range

get.summary: GET /api/insights/trends - Get time-series trend data for charts.

200.description: Time-series trend data for metrics and heatmap

get.summary: GET /api/invocables - List all invocables with their usage counts.
get.description:
Returns a list of all known invocables (tools, skills, MCPs) ordered by
invocation count descending, then name ascending.

200.description: All invocables (tools, skills, MCPs) sorted by usage

get.summary: GET /api/jobs — List all active jobs.
GET /api/jobs/stream — SSE stream of all job progress updates.
GET /api/live/pricing -- Return the model pricing table.
get.description: Exposes per-model costs in a frontend-friendly format (cost per million tokens).

200.description: Model pricing table with per-token costs

delete.summary: DELETE /api/live/recently-closed -- Dismiss all recently closed (in-memory only).

get.summary: GET /api/live/sessions -- List all live sessions, sorted by most recent activity.
GET /api/live/sessions/:id -- Get a single live session by ID.

post.summary: POST /api/live/sessions/:id/bind-control -- Sidecar notifies that it now controls this session.
post.description:
Sets the `control` field on the LiveSession, which flows to SSE clients.
Idempotent: re-binding with the same controlId is a no-op success.

delete.summary: DELETE /api/live/sessions/:id/dismiss -- Dismiss from recently closed (in-memory only).

404.description: Session not found in recently closed

post.summary: POST /api/live/sessions/:id/kill -- Send SIGTERM to the session's Claude process.
post.description:
Returns `{ "killed": true, "pid": <pid> }` on success.
Returns 500 with `{ "error": "...", "pid": <pid> }` if SIGTERM fails.
Returns 404 with `{ "canDismiss": true }` if the session has no PID (already exited).
Returns 404 with `{ "error": "Session not found" }` if the session ID is unknown.

404.description: Session not found or already exited

get.summary:
GET /api/live/sessions/:id/messages?limit=20 -- Get the most recent messages
for a live session.
get.description:
Looks up the session in the live_sessions map to find its JSONL file path,
then parses the file and returns the last N messages (most recent).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of messages to return (default: 20).

200.description: Recent messages from a live session

get.summary: GET /api/live/sessions/{id}/statusline -- debug endpoint returning raw statusline JSON.

200.description: Raw statusline JSON for a live session

404.description: Session not found or no statusline data

post.summary: POST /api/live/sessions/:id/unbind-control -- Sidecar notifies it released control.
post.description: Only unbinds if the current controlId matches (CAS semantics).

200.description: Statusline data accepted and applied to live session

get.summary: GET /api/live/stream -- SSE stream of real-time session events.
get.description:
# Events

| Event name            | When emitted                           |
|-----------------------|----------------------------------------|
| `snapshot`            | On connect and lag recovery (full state)|
| `session_upsert`      | Session created or updated             |
| `session_remove`      | Session removed from active map        |
| `heartbeat`           | Every 15 seconds to keep connection    |

On initial connection the server sends a single `snapshot` event containing
the summary, all active sessions, and recently closed sessions so the client
can hydrate immediately without a separate REST call.

200.description: SSE stream of live session events

get.summary: GET /api/live/summary -- Aggregate statistics across all live sessions.
GET /api/mcp-servers — returns all deduplicated MCP server configurations.
GET /api/memory — returns all memory entries grouped by scope.
GET /api/memory/file?path=... — read a single memory file.
GET /api/memory/:project — returns memories for a specific project.
GET /api/monitor/snapshot -- One-shot JSON snapshot of current resources.

200.description: Current resource snapshot (CPU, memory, disk, processes)

get.summary: GET /api/monitor/stream -- SSE stream of periodic resource snapshots.
get.description:
# Lazy observer pattern

On first subscriber (0 → 1), spawns a background polling task that collects
system resources every 2 seconds. On last unsubscribe (1 → 0), the polling
task exits on its next tick. This avoids wasting CPU when no one is watching.

# Events

| Event name  | When emitted                       |
|-------------|------------------------------------|
| `init`      | On connect: system info + snapshot  |
| `snapshot`  | Every 2s: resource snapshot          |
| `heartbeat` | Every 15s: keepalive                 |

200.description: SSE stream of resource snapshots (CPU, memory, disk)

get.summary: `GET /api/oauth/identity`
get.description:
Returns cached auth identity (email, org, plan).
Calls `claude auth status` on first request only, caches forever.

get.summary: `GET /api/oauth/usage`
get.description:
Returns cached usage data (5-min TTL). Sets `Cache-Control: max-age=<ttl>`
so the frontend's polling interval follows the server's TTL.

200.description: OAuth usage tiers with cache headers

post.summary: `POST /api/oauth/usage/refresh`
post.description:
Bypass TTL cache and fetch fresh data. Returns 429 + `Retry-After` if
called within 60s of the last attempt.

429.description: Rate limited, retry after header set

get.summary: GET /api/pairing/qr — Generate a QR payload via Supabase pair-offer.
GET /api/plugins -- Unified view of installed + available plugins.
GET /api/plugins/marketplaces

post.summary: POST /api/plugins/marketplaces/action

400.description: Invalid action or missing required fields

409.description: A mutation is already in progress

post.summary: POST /api/plugins/marketplaces/refresh-all
post.description:
Starts a batch refresh of all (or specified) marketplaces.
Returns 409 if a batch is already active.

get.summary: GET /api/plugins/marketplaces/refresh-status
GET /api/plugins/ops — List all queued/running/completed ops.

post.summary: POST /api/plugins/ops — Enqueue a plugin mutation, return immediately.

get.summary: GET /api/projects — list all projects backed by the in-memory catalog.
get.description:
`is_archived` is derived from filesystem existence of the project directory —
if the dir has been deleted, the project is flagged archived. This matches
the old DB-backed behavior exactly; the SQL query used `COUNT dir_exists`.

get.summary: GET /api/projects/:id/branches - List distinct branches with session counts.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Project ID or git root path (URL-encoded)

get.summary: GET /api/projects/:id/sessions — paginated sessions for one project.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Project ID or git root path (URL-encoded)

parameters.name: branch
parameters.description:
Accepted for API compatibility but not yet honored — branch filtering
requires per-session JSONL parse + git-branch derivation. Deferred.

parameters.name: includeSidechains
parameters.description: Accepted for API compatibility; the catalog already excludes sidechains.

get.summary: GET /api/prompts - List prompt history with optional search/filter.
GET /api/prompts/stats - Aggregate prompt statistics.
GET /api/prompts/templates - Detected prompt templates.

200.description: Detected prompt templates with slot patterns

get.summary: GET /api/reports — List all saved reports.

post.summary: POST /api/reports/generate — Stream-generate a report via Claude CLI.
post.description:
Returns SSE with events:
- `chunk` — text chunk from Claude CLI stdout
- `done`  — generation complete, includes `{ reportId }` JSON
- `error` — generation failed, includes `{ message }` JSON

get.summary: GET /api/reports/preview — Aggregate preview stats for a date range.
GET /api/reports/:id — Get a single report.
GET /api/score - Get the current AI Fluency Score.
get.description:
Returns a composite score (0-100) plus sub-metric breakdown
computed from session facets.

200.description: Composite AI fluency score with sub-metric breakdown

get.summary: GET /api/search - Search raw session JSONL files with grep.
GET /api/sessions — list sessions with filter/sort/search.
get.description:
See the module docstring for the pipeline. See
[`SessionsListQuery`](super::types::SessionsListQuery) for the full set
of supported parameters.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Filter: all (default), has_commits, high_reedit, long_session (kept for backward compat)

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort: recent (default), tokens, prompts, files_edited, duration

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Text search across preview, last_message, project name

parameters.name: branches
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of branches to filter by

parameters.name: models
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of models to filter by

parameters.name: has_commits
parameters.description: Filter sessions with commits (true) or without (false)

parameters.name: has_skills
parameters.description: Filter sessions with skills (true) or without (false)

parameters.name: high_reedit
parameters.description: Filter sessions with high re-edit rate (> 0.2)

parameters.name: time_after
parameters.description: Filter sessions after this timestamp (unix seconds)

parameters.name: time_before
parameters.description: Filter sessions before this timestamp (unix seconds)

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: Optional project filter (matches project_id or git_root)

parameters.name: show_archived
parameters.description: Include archived sessions (queries `sessions` table instead of `valid_sessions` view)

get.summary: GET /api/sessions/activity — Activity histogram for sparkline chart.

200.description: Activity histogram with date buckets and session count

get.summary: GET /api/sessions/activity/rich — Full server-side activity aggregation.
get.description:
Returns histogram, project breakdown, and summary stats in a single request.
Replaces the client-side `useActivityData` pagination loop.

get.summary: GET /api/sessions/:id — extended session detail.
get.description:
Pipeline (same shape as the list endpoint, scoped to one id):
  1. `SessionCatalog::get` — id → file metadata.
  2. `session_stats::extract_stats` — JSONL on-demand compute.
  3. `enrichment::fetch_enrichments` — bulk DB layer (archived, commits_count,
     skills, reedit_rate). Single-id call here; same layer as list path.
  4. `get_commits_for_session` — per-session linked commit detail (full tier +
     evidence), not derivable from JSONL so still DB-backed.
  5. Read task/todo/plan files from `~/.claude/{tasks,todos,plans}`.

200.description: Session detail with commits and derived metrics

get.summary: GET /api/sessions/:id/file-history — List all file changes for a session.

200.description: File change history for the session

get.summary: GET /api/sessions/:id/file-history/:file_hash/diff?from=N&to=M

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.description:
If provided, skip JSONL re-parse and use this path directly.
The frontend already has the resolved path from the file-history response.

400.description: Invalid file hash or version numbers

get.summary: GET /api/sessions/:id/hook-events — Fetch hook events for a session.
get.description:
For live sessions, hook events are in memory (not flushed to SQLite until
SessionEnd). Check the live state first, then fall back to SQLite.

**Phase 3 PR 3.5 — no cutover.** Hook events live on the
`hook_events` table (Channel B per design decision D8 of
`2026-04-17-cqrs-phase-1-7-design.md` §1). Channel B is
authoritative for real-time agent state, and `session_stats` is
authoritative for parsed message stats — they carry different data
and never dedup against each other (see CLAUDE.md "Separate
Channels = Separate Data"). This handler is therefore unchanged by
Phase 3; the openapi_compatibility test pins the response shape.

get.summary: GET /api/sessions/:id/messages — Get paginated messages by session ID.
get.description:
Resolves the JSONL file path from the DB's `file_path` column.
No `project_dir` parameter needed — the server owns path resolution.

200.description: Paginated session messages (block or legacy format)

get.summary: GET /api/sessions/:id/parsed — Get full parsed session by ID.
get.description:
Resolves the JSONL file path from the DB's `file_path` column.
No `project_dir` parameter needed — the server owns path resolution.

get.summary: GET /api/sessions/:id/plans -- returns plan documents for the session's slug.
get.description:
Plans are a Layer 3 concern (per
design §5 of `2026-04-17-cqrs-phase-1-7-design.md`): filesystem
files under `~/.claude/plans/<slug>/*.md` keyed by the session's
slug. Phase 7.h carries that slug on `session_stats`, and the
openapi_compatibility test pins this endpoint's response shape.

200.description: Plan documents associated with the session

404.description: Session not found or has no plan slug

get.summary:
GET /api/sessions/:id/rich — Parse JSONL on demand via `SessionAccumulator` and return
rich session data (tokens, cost, cache status, sub-agents, progress items, etc.).
get.description:
This endpoint bridges historical sessions with the same rich data shape used by
Live Monitor, enabling a unified session detail view.

200.description: Rich parsed session data with tokens, cost, and sub-agents

get.summary: GET /api/sessions/:id/subagents/:agent_id/messages — Paginated blocks for a sub-agent.
get.description:
Resolves the parent session's JSONL path, then derives the sub-agent's JSONL
file using the same path convention as the terminal WebSocket handler.
Returns `PaginatedBlocks` — same shape as the parent messages endpoint.

get.summary: GET /api/sessions/{id}/turns -- Per-turn breakdown for a historical session.

200.description: Per-turn breakdown with wall-clock and CC durations

post.summary: Resolve a pending interaction (permission, question, plan, elicitation).
post.description:
1. Parse InteractRequest from body
2. Look up session in live_sessions — 404 if not found
3. Resolve ownership — 400 if Observed
4. Check interaction_data side-map — 409 if request_id not found (stale)
5. For SDK: forward to sidecar HTTP API
6. Clear pending interaction via coordinator

409.description: Request ID stale or already resolved

get.summary: Fetch the full interaction data for a session's pending interaction.
get.description:
1. Look up session in live_sessions — 404 if not found
2. Read pending_interaction — 404 if None (no pending interaction)
3. Look up full data in interaction_data side-map — 404 if not found

**Phase 3 PR 3.7 — no cutover.** Interaction data is live-only state
(pending-interaction + interaction_data side-map) maintained by the
live session manager. It is not derivable from JSONL or
`session_stats` — those reflect the historical record, while this
endpoint serves in-flight permission/question/plan prompts. The
2026-04-17 design doc §5.1 contemplated using
`session_stats.last_ts` for cache validation, but the current
handler has no cache surface that would benefit. Response shape is
pinned by the openapi_compatibility test.

get.summary: GET /api/settings - Read current app settings.

put.summary: PUT /api/settings/git-sync-interval - Update the git sync interval.

get.summary: GET /api/stats/ai-generation - AI generation statistics with time range filtering.
get.description:
Query params:
- `from`: Period start (Unix timestamp, optional)
- `to`: Period end (Unix timestamp, optional)

Returns:
- linesAdded, linesRemoved: Currently not tracked, returns 0 (future migration needed)
- filesCreated: Files edited/created by AI
- totalInputTokens, totalOutputTokens: Aggregate token usage
- tokensByModel: Token breakdown by AI model
- tokensByProject: Top 5 projects by token usage + "Others"

parameters.name: from
parameters.description:
Period start timestamp (Unix seconds, inclusive).
If omitted along with `to`, returns all-time stats with no trends.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description:
Period end timestamp (Unix seconds, inclusive).
If omitted along with `from`, returns all-time stats with no trends.

200.description: AI generation statistics (tokens, cost, tool usage)

get.summary: GET /api/stats/dashboard - Pre-computed dashboard statistics with time range filtering.
get.description:
Query params:
- `from`: Period start (Unix timestamp, optional)
- `to`: Period end (Unix timestamp, optional)

If `from` and `to` are omitted, returns all-time stats with no trends.
If provided, returns stats filtered to that period with comparison to the equivalent previous period.

Returns:
- Base stats: total_sessions, total_projects, heatmap, top_skills, top_projects, tool_totals
- Current period: session_count, total_tokens, total_files_edited, commit_count
- Trends: period-over-period changes for key metrics (None if viewing all-time)
- Period bounds: periodStart, periodEnd, comparisonPeriodStart, comparisonPeriodEnd
- dataStartDate: earliest session in database

parameters.name: from
parameters.description:
Period start timestamp (Unix seconds, inclusive).
If omitted along with `to`, returns all-time stats with no trends.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description:
Period end timestamp (Unix seconds, inclusive).
If omitted along with `from`, returns all-time stats with no trends.

200.description: Dashboard statistics with trends and heatmap

get.summary: GET /api/stats/overview - Aggregate usage statistics.
get.description:
Returns total sessions, total invocations, unique invocables used,
and the top 10 invocables by usage count.

200.description: Aggregate usage stats (sessions, invocations, top invocables)

get.summary: GET /api/stats/storage - Storage statistics for the settings page.
get.description:
Returns:
- Storage sizes: JSONL files, SQLite database, obsolete search index cache
- Counts: sessions, projects, commits
- Timing: oldest session, last index, last git sync

get.summary: GET /api/stats/tokens - Aggregate token usage statistics.

200.description: Aggregate token usage with cache ratios

get.summary: GET /api/status - Get index metadata and data freshness info.
get.description:
Returns:
- last_indexed_at: When indexing last completed
- last_index_duration_ms: How long the last index took
- sessions_indexed: Number of sessions in last index
- projects_indexed: Number of projects in last index
- last_git_sync_at: When git sync last completed
- commits_found: Commits found in last git sync
- links_created: Session-commit links created in last git sync
- updated_at: When metadata was last updated

post.summary: POST /api/sync/deep - Trigger a full deep index rebuild.
post.description:
This endpoint:
1. Marks all sessions for re-indexing (clears deep_indexed_at)
2. Runs Pass 2 deep indexing on all sessions

Returns:
- 202 Accepted: Deep index rebuild started
- 409 Conflict: A rebuild is already in progress

The rebuild runs in the background. Poll /api/status or /api/indexing/progress for completion.
POST /api/sync/deep-index — Trigger a full deep index rebuild.

post.summary: POST /api/sync/git - Trigger git commit scanning (A8.5).
post.description:
Returns:
- 202 Accepted: Sync started (no sync was running)
- 409 Conflict: Sync already in progress

The sync runs in the background. Poll /api/status for completion.

get.summary: GET /api/sync/git/progress - SSE stream of git sync progress.
get.description:
# Events

| Event name     | When emitted                     |
|----------------|----------------------------------|
| `scanning`     | Repos being scanned              |
| `correlating`  | Linking commits to sessions      |
| `done`         | Sync complete                    |
| `error`        | Sync failed                      |

The stream terminates after `done` or `error`.
GET /api/sync/git/progress — SSE stream of git sync progress.

200.description: SSE stream of git sync progress events

get.summary: GET /api/system - Get comprehensive system status.
get.description:
Runs storage, health, and classification queries in parallel
using tokio::join!, then detects Claude CLI status.

200.description: Comprehensive system status including storage, health, and classification

post.summary: POST /api/system/clear-cache - Clear obsolete search cache.
post.description:
Session search is grep-only now, so this endpoint only removes any obsolete
session-search cache left by older versions. It does not recreate an index.

post.summary: POST /api/system/git-resync - Trigger full git re-sync.
POST /api/system/reindex - Trigger a full re-index.
post.description:
This is a lightweight endpoint that signals the server to start
a background re-index. The actual work happens asynchronously.

post.summary: POST /api/system/reset - Factory reset all data.
post.description: Requires a confirmation string "RESET_ALL_DATA" in the request body.

get.summary: GET /api/teams — List all teams.
GET /api/teams/:name — Get team detail.
get.description: Enriches inbox-augmented members with model info from the lead JSONL.

get.summary: GET /api/teams/:name/cost — Get team cost breakdown.
get.description:
Resolves team member session IDs from the lead session's JSONL, then runs
`SessionAccumulator::from_file` on each member session to compute per-member costs.

get.summary: GET /api/teams/:name/inbox — Get team inbox messages.
GET /api/teams/:name/sidechains?session_id=xxx — Get team member sidechains.
get.description:
Resolves sidechain `.meta.json` / `.jsonl` pairs inside the session directory
to enumerate each member's spawned sub-conversations.

post.summary: POST /api/telemetry/consent — Set telemetry consent preference.

get.summary: GET /api/trends - Get week-over-week trend metrics.
get.description:
Returns trends for:
- Session count
- Total tokens
- Avg tokens per prompt
- Total files edited
- Avg re-edit rate
- Commit link count

200.description: Week-over-week trend metrics for sessions, tokens, files, commits

get.summary: GET /api/webhooks — list all webhooks (secrets excluded).

post.summary: POST /api/webhooks — create a new webhook (returns signing secret once).

400.description: Invalid request (bad URL, name, or events)

500.description: Failed to persist webhook config or secrets

get.summary: GET /api/webhooks/{id} — get a single webhook by ID.

put.summary: PUT /api/webhooks/{id} — update an existing webhook (partial update).

delete.summary: DELETE /api/webhooks/{id} — remove a webhook from config and secrets.

500.description: Failed to persist config or secrets

post.summary: POST /api/webhooks/{id}/test — send a synthetic test payload.

500.description: Signing secret not found or delivery error

post.summary: POST /api/workflows/chat — direct POST-to-SSE streaming (same pattern as generate_report())

cost.description: Aggregate cost breakdown (computed server-side using pricing engine).

AIGenerationStatsResponse.description: AI generation response wrapper with additive metadata.

ActionResponse.description: Generic action response for POST endpoints.

ActiveSession.description:
A parsed session file from ~/.claude/sessions/{pid}.json.

Written by Claude Code CLI — external data we don't control.
Every field uses `#[serde(default)]` so missing fields never
cause silent deserialization failures.

cwd.description: Working directory where the session was started.

name.description:
Optional session name (from `claude --name "foo"`).
Present when user starts session with explicit name.

pid.description: Process ID of the Claude Code process.

startedAt.description: Unix timestamp (milliseconds) when the session started.

ActivityPoint.description: A single point in the activity histogram.

totalSeconds.description: Total duration in seconds for this bucket (used by CalendarHeatmap).

ActivitySummaryRow.description: Summary stats for the activity page.

AgentTodos.description: A todo file parsed with its session and agent context.

agentId.description: Agent UUID (same as session_id for main agent, different for subagents).

isMainAgent.description: Whether this is the main agent (session_id == agent_id).

items.description: The todo items (only non-empty results are returned).

sessionId.description: Session UUID this todo belongs to.

AggregateCostBreakdown.description: Aggregate cost breakdown in USD (computed from per-model token data + pricing engine).

computedPricedTotalCostUsd.description: Sum of cost components computed from models that had a pricing match.

hasUnpricedUsage.description: True when any usage lacked model pricing.

pricedModelCount.description: Number of models in the query window that had an exact/prefix pricing match.

pricedTokenCoverage.description: Fraction of all tokens that were priced with real model rates [0.0, 1.0].

unpricedInputTokens.description: Tokens from unpriced models (surfaced explicitly; never converted via fallback rates).

unpricedModelCount.description: Number of models in the query window without pricing.

AvailablePlugin.description: A plugin available for installation (not yet installed).

BenchmarksResponseWithMeta.description: Benchmarks response wrapper with additive metadata.

BindControlRequest.description: Request body for binding sidecar control to a session.

BranchSession.description: Lightweight session summary for branch expansion.

avgReeditRate.description: Average re-edit rate (files re-edited / files edited)

CategoryPerformance.description: Performance metrics for a single category.

vsAverage.description: Difference from user's overall average (negative = better)

CategorySummary.description: Count and percentage for a single L1 category.

ClassifyLastRun.description: Information about the last completed classification run.

ClassifyProgressInfo.description: Progress information for a running classification.

wasCached.description: true if result was already cached (previously classified)

path.description: Path to claude binary, None if not found.

version.description: Version string, None if not found.

ClaudeCodeSettings.description: Merged, redacted Claude Code settings for display.

systemHooks.description: System hooks (claude-view) — only included when expanded

CliSession.description:
A tmux-managed CLI session running Claude Code.
Used by reconciliation at startup.

claudeSessionId.description:
The Claude session UUID running inside this tmux pane.
Resolved lazily from ~/.claude/sessions/{pane_pid}.json.

createdAt.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when the session was created.

projectDir.description: Working directory for the CLI session.

CoachingRule.description: A coaching rule parsed from a `coaching-*.md` file.

CommitWithTier.description: A commit linked to a session with its confidence tier

tier.description: Tier 1 = high confidence (commit skill), Tier 2 = medium (during session)

systemMonitor.description: Whether the system resource monitor is enabled.

anonymousId.description: Anonymous device ID for telemetry — null when running self-hosted.

auth.description: Whether Supabase auth is configured (JWKS loaded).

posthogKey.description: PostHog project API key — null when running self-hosted (no compiled key).

sharing.description: Whether conversation sharing is configured (Worker + Viewer URLs set).

telemetry.description: Current telemetry opt-in status (disabled when no PostHog key compiled in).

ContributionWarning.description: Warning attached to response when data is incomplete.

hasUnpricedUsage.description: True when any tokens were excluded from USD due to missing model pricing.

pricedTokenCoverage.description: Fraction of all tokens priced with real model rates [0.0, 1.0].

unpricedInputTokens.description: Tokens excluded from USD totals (no pricing match).

args.description: Additional CLI args to pass to `claude`.

projectDir.description: Optional working directory for the CLI session.

CreateResponse.description:
Response for POST /api/cli-sessions.

Returns the real Claude session UUID (resolved by polling pid.json)
and the tmux session name (needed for DELETE and terminal attachment).

CurrentPeriodMetrics.description: Current period metrics for dashboard (adapts to selected time range).

DailyTrendPoint.description: Daily trend data point for charts.

DayActivity.description: A single day in the activity heatmap.

DerivedMetrics.description: Derived metrics calculated from atomic units

DeviceRow.description:
Minimal device row shape the Mac daemon needs. Fields align 1:1 with
`public.devices`.

Per feedback_external_data_serde_default.md every field is `#[serde(default)]`.

ed25519_pubkey.description:
Phase 2 Task 8: widened so relay_client can encrypt for the peer.
PostgREST default emits `\x<hex>` for BYTEA; callers normalise.

EffectiveRangeMeta.description: Canonical resolved range metadata returned additively by API responses.

EffectiveRangeSource.description: Canonical source for how a request's effective time range was resolved.

EffectivenessMetrics.description: Effectiveness metrics for the overview card.

hasUnpricedUsage.description: True when any tokens were excluded from cost because model pricing was missing.

pricedTokenCoverage.description: Fraction of tokens priced with real model rates [0.0, 1.0].

EnvVar.description: Environment variable entry (value may be redacted).

ExportedSession.description: Exported session data for JSON format (A5.2 schema).

comparisonPeriodEnd.description: End of the comparison period (Unix timestamp).

comparisonPeriodStart.description: Start of the comparison period (Unix timestamp).

currentWeek.description: Current period metrics (adapts to selected time range)

dataStartDate.description:
Earliest session date in the database (Unix timestamp).
Used to display "since [date]" in the UI.

periodEnd.description: End of the requested period (Unix timestamp).

periodStart.description: Start of the requested period (Unix timestamp).

ExtendedDashboardStats.description: Extended dashboard stats with current period and trends.

FileImpact.description: File impact for session detail view.

FluencyMetrics.description: Fluency metrics for the overview card.

endTs.description: Unix timestamp for range end (from frontend, uses local midnight + 86399).

startTs.description: Unix timestamp for range start (from frontend, uses local midnight).

GeneratedInsight.description: A generated insight with human-readable text and scoring.

HealthResponse.description: Response for the health check endpoint.

HealthStatus.description: Health status enum for the system page.

HookMatcher.description: A hook matcher group from settings.json.

allOf.description: Safety net: capture any fields Claude Code adds in the future.

IndexMetadata.description: Index metadata for data freshness tracking.

commitsFound.description: Number of commits found in last git sync.

gitSyncIntervalSecs.description: User-configurable git sync interval in seconds (default 60).

lastGitSyncAt.description: Unix timestamp of last successful git sync.

lastIndexDurationMs.description: Duration of last successful index in milliseconds.

lastIndexedAt.description: Unix timestamp of last successful index completion.

linksCreated.description: Number of session-commit links created in last git sync.

projectsIndexed.description: Number of projects indexed in last run.

sessionsIndexed.description: Number of sessions indexed in last run.

updatedAt.description: Unix timestamp of last metadata update.

IndexRunInfo.description: Index history entry in system response.

IndexingStatusResponse.description: JSON snapshot of current indexing progress (for polling).

Insight.description: A single insight with optional severity/type.

kind.description: Insight type for styling (info, success, warning, tip).

bucket.description: Bucket granularity that was actually used (input clamped).

effectiveRange.description: Resolved `[from, to)` unix range that was queried.

legacyPath.description:
Always `false` — the legacy GROUP BY fallback was retired in
CQRS Phase 7.g. Kept on the wire for client compatibility and
will be removed in a separate release.

rowsRead.description: Rows read from the rollup table before aggregation.

InsightsOverview.description: Overview statistics for the insights page.

InsightsTrendsResponse.description: Full trends response wrapper with additive metadata.

IntegrityCounterInfo.description: Integrity counter values from the latest index run.

InteractRequest.description: Request body for the interact endpoint. Tagged by `variant`.

InvocableWithCount.description: An invocable (tool/skill/MCP) with its aggregated invocation count.

source.description: For add: GitHub repo URL or owner/repo

McpServer.description: A deduplicated MCP server for API response.

hasOauth.description: Whether this server uses OAuth authentication.

needsReauth.description: Whether this server needs re-authentication (from auth cache).

serverType.description: Transport type: "http", "stdio", or null if unknown.

McpServerIndex.description: Summary of all discovered MCP servers.

MemoryEntry.description: A single memory entry parsed from a .md file.

projectDir.description: Encoded project dir name (empty for global)

MemoryIndex.description: Summary of all memories grouped by scope.

MemoryType.description: Memory type enum matching Claude Code's 4 memory types.

MetricDataPoint.description: Time-series data point for metric trends.

ModelInsight.description: One model's aggregated usage for the requested time range.

avgDurationSeconds.description:
Mean session duration in seconds (computed from
`duration_sum_ms / duration_count / 1000`). Zero when no
sessions in the range contributed a duration.

promptCount.description: Sum of user-prompt counts across sessions.

sessionCount.description: Sessions that had this as their primary model.

totalTokens.description: Sum of input + output + cache-read + cache-creation tokens.

ModelStats.description: Model statistics for the byModel breakdown.

ModelWithStats.description: A model record with aggregated usage stats (for GET /api/models).

filePath.description: Relative path to the file within the project (optional).

ide.description: The IDE id to open (must match an id from the detect response).

projectPath.description: Absolute path to the project directory.

OutputMetrics.description: Output metrics for the overview card.

OverallAverages.description: Overall averages across all sessions for comparison.

OverviewMetrics.description: Overview section combining all three metric cards.

PairedDeviceResponse.description:
Legacy schema kept for OpenAPI component compatibility. No routes emit it
anymore — devices live at /api/devices after Phase 2.

lastIndexDurationMs.description: Duration of last successful index in milliseconds.

sessionsPerSec.description: Sessions indexed per second during last index.

throughputBytesPerSec.description: Throughput: bytes processed per second during last index.

PeriodMetrics.description: Metrics for a time period (used in Then vs Now comparison).

reeditRate.description: Re-edit rate: files re-edited / files edited (0.0-1.0)

variant.description: Variant suffix if any (e.g. "IMPROVEMENTS", "gaps"), None for the base plan

name.description: Plugin name or full ID (e.g. "superpowers" or "superpowers@marketplace")

projectPath.description:
For project-scoped plugins: the project directory where it was installed.
Required for uninstall of project-scoped plugins (CLI needs correct CWD).

PluginInfo.description: An installed plugin with its metadata, items, and usage stats.

description.description: Description from the marketplace listing (mirrors AvailablePlugin).

installCount.description: Global install count from the marketplace listing (mirrors AvailablePlugin).

sourceExists.description:
True when the plugin's install directory exists on disk.
False = truly orphaned (files deleted/moved).
True + errors = CLI validation failure (catalog mismatch), but files are intact.

PluginItem.description: A single invocable item within a plugin (skill, command, agent, or MCP tool).

content.description: Full file content for the item (markdown for skills/commands/agents; pretty JSON for mcp_tool)

PluginsResponse.description: Full response for the plugins endpoint.

cliError.description: Non-empty when the CLI call failed -- used by PluginHealthBanner.

user_id.description:
UUID from the JWT's `sub` claim. Web UI reads this from
supabase.auth.getUser() and forwards it so we don't double-parse.

ProcessGroup.description: A single process group — aggregated by normalized process name.

cpuPercent.description: Total CPU usage across all processes in this group (0.0–N cores).

memoryBytes.description: Total memory in bytes (physical footprint on macOS, RSS elsewhere).

name.description: Normalized display name (e.g. "Google Chrome", not "Google Chrome Helper (Renderer)").

processCount.description: Number of OS processes in this group.

ProgressComparison.description: Progress comparison between first and last month.

ProjectActivityRow.description: Project-level aggregation for the activity page.

ProjectInsight.description: One project's aggregated usage for the requested time range.

commitCount.description: Distinct commit count attributed to this project.

linesAdded.description:
AI-attributed lines added — populated from the PR 4.8
contribution_snapshots fold for history, and from Phase 5
SessionFlags going forward.

ProjectMemoryGroup.description: A group of memories for one project.

ProjectStat.description: A project with its session count (for dashboard top projects).

ProjectSummary.description: Lightweight project summary for sidebar — no sessions array.

isArchived.description: Whether the project directory no longer exists on disk.

snippet.description:
HTML snippet with `<b>` tags around matched search terms.
`None` in browse/filter-only mode (no free-text query).

QrPayload.description:
QR payload served to the web UI. The `url` field is the QR-encoded value;
the rest are duplicates for frontends that prefer named fields.

expires_at.description: ISO-8601 expiry timestamp — UI shows the countdown.

r.description: Relay WebSocket URL (so the phone knows where to connect after claim).

t.description: One-time pairing token. Sent to `pair-claim` edge fn by the phone.

url.description: Mobile URL — what the QR encodes. Points at the claim page with `?token=...`.

v.description: Protocol version (2 — HMAC removed in Phase 2).

ReportPreview.description: Preview stats for a date range (no AI, pure DB aggregation).

hasUnpricedUsage.description: True when one or more sessions in range have unpriced usage.

totalCostCents.description: Sum of priced session costs only (sessions with NULL total_cost_usd are excluded).

hasUnpricedUsage.description: True when some sessions in this period had unpriced usage (NULL total_cost_usd).

ResourceSnapshot.description: A periodic resource snapshot broadcast to SSE clients.

diskTotalBytes.description: Total disk space in bytes. Absent on fast ticks.

diskUsedBytes.description:
Used disk space in bytes. Absent on fast ticks (2s cadence) — only
computed on full ticks (10s). Consumer must carry forward last known.

sessionResources.description: Per-session resource usage for active Claude sessions.

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) when this snapshot was taken.

topProcesses.description:
Top processes by CPU+memory, grouped by normalized name. Absent on fast
ticks — only computed on full ticks (10s). Consumer must carry forward.

RichActivityResponse.description: Full server-side activity response (replaces client-side aggregation).

total.description:
True total from valid_sessions (includes sessions with last_message_at=0
that can't be placed on the chart axis).

SessionBadge.description: Per-session badge data returned in the badges map.

hasPlans.description: Whether plan files exist for this session's slug

warnings.description: Warnings for non-fatal data read failures (e.g. task/plan file errors)

SessionDetail.description: Extended session detail with commits (for GET /api/sessions/:id)

SessionDurationStat.description: A session entry for the "Longest Tasks" dashboard card.

SessionInfo.description: Session info for listing (without full message content)

displayName.description:
Human-readable project name (e.g. "claude-backup"), derived from CWD
evidence at index time. When no CWD is available, falls back to the
raw encoded directory name (no guessing).

gitRoot.description:
Canonical git repository root resolved from session_cwd at index time.
None for non-git directories or sessions indexed before this field existed.

SessionResource.description: Per-session resource snapshot (CPU + memory for the Claude process).

cpuPercent.description: CPU usage of this process (0.0–100.0 per core).

memoryBytes.description: Memory in bytes (physical footprint on macOS, RSS elsewhere).

impactOnReedit.description: Percentage improvement in re-edit rate after adoption

SkillStat.description: A skill with its usage count.

StatsOverview.description: Aggregate statistics overview for the API.

context_window_size.description:
The real context window limit in tokens (200_000 or 1_000_000).
This is the authoritative value -- no guessing needed.

oneOf.description:
Current turn token usage breakdown. Claude Code sends this as an object
with per-category token counts, not a single integer.

total_input_tokens.description: Cumulative input tokens across the session.

total_output_tokens.description: Cumulative output tokens across the session.

used_percentage.description: Pre-computed percentage used (0.0-100.0). Null early in session.

total_cost_usd.description: Claude Code's own total cost calculation in USD.

total_duration_ms.description: Total wall-clock session duration in milliseconds.

total_lines_added.description: Total lines added across the session.

total_lines_removed.description: Total lines removed across the session.

StorageStats.description: Storage statistics for the settings page.

appDataPath.description: Parent app data directory — safe to delete, rebuilt on next launch.

indexBytes.description: Size of obsolete session search cache in bytes.

indexPath.description: Path to obsolete session search cache directory.

jsonlBytes.description: Size of JSONL session files in bytes.

jsonlPath.description: Path to JSONL session files (Claude Code data, read-only).

lastGitSyncAt.description: Unix timestamp of last git sync.

lastGitSyncDurationMs.description: Duration of last git sync in milliseconds (not currently tracked, returns None).

lastGitSyncRepoCount.description: Number of repos scanned in last git sync (not currently tracked, returns 0).

lastIndexAt.description: Unix timestamp of last index completion.

lastIndexDurationMs.description: Duration of last index in milliseconds.

lastIndexSessionCount.description: Number of sessions indexed in last run.

sqliteBytes.description: Size of SQLite database in bytes.

SyncStatus.description: Status value for accepted sync responses.

SystemInfo.description: Static system information that doesn't change between snapshots.

TaskItem.description:
A persistent task item from ~/.claude/tasks/{sessionId}/{id}.json.

Written by Claude Code CLI — external data we don't control.
Every field uses `#[serde(default)]` so unknown/missing fields never
cause silent deserialization failures.

TeamCostBreakdown.description: Aggregated cost breakdown for an entire team.

TeamMemberCost.description: Per-member cost data for the team cost breakdown.

oneOf.description: Full cost breakdown (None if session not found).

costUsd.description: Total cost in USD (None if session not found or not yet resolved).

inProcess.description: True when member runs in-process -- cost is included in the coordinator total.

sessionId.description: Resolved session ID (None for in-process members whose cost is in the lead session).

oneOf.description: Token usage breakdown (None if session not found).

TeamMemberSidechain.description: A single sidechain instance for a team member.

costUsd.description: Cost in USD (computed from JSONL token usage + pricing).

durationSeconds.description: Duration in seconds (derived from started_at -> ended_at).

endedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the last JSONL entry.

lineCount.description: Number of JSONL lines (proxy for amount of work done).

startedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the first JSONL entry.

oneOf.description: Token usage breakdown (input, output, cache read, cache creation).

TierKind.description: How the frontend should group a tier in the tooltip.

TodoItem.description:
A single todo item from a TodoWrite output file.

Written by Claude Code CLI — external data we don't control.
Every field uses `#[serde(default)]` so missing fields never
cause silent deserialization failures.

activeForm.description: Human-readable description of the current work on this todo.

id.description: Optional unique ID (present in ~8% of items).

priority.description: Optional priority level (present in ~1% of items).

TokenUsage.description: Accumulated token counts for a live session.

cacheCreation1hrTokens.description: Cache creation tokens with 1-hour TTL (from JSONL ephemeral_1h_input_tokens).

cacheCreation5mTokens.description: Cache creation tokens with 5-minute TTL (from JSONL ephemeral_5m_input_tokens).

ToolCounts.description: Tool usage statistics for a session

TrendMetric.description: A single trend metric comparing current vs previous period.

deltaPercent.description:
Percentage change, rounded to 1 decimal place.
None if previous == 0 (cannot calculate percentage).

TurnInfo.description: A single turn in the session breakdown.

ccDurationMs.description:
Claude Code reported turn duration in milliseconds (from `turn_duration` system message).
Null if no `turn_duration` message follows this turn.

promptPreview.description: First 60 characters of the user prompt text.

startedAt.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) when the turn started (user prompt).

wallClockSeconds.description: Wall-clock seconds from turn start to turn end (last message before next turn or EOF).

UnbindControlRequest.description: Request body for unbinding sidecar control from a session.

UpdateGitSyncIntervalRequest.description: Request body for updating the git sync interval.

intervalSecs.description: Interval in seconds. Must be between 10 and 3600.

UsageTier.description:
One pill row in the usage tooltip.

**Backward compat:** all new fields are optional or have a stable default.
`kind` was added 2026-04-30 alongside the new tier set; older clients will
read it as a string they don't recognise and just render the row generically.

currency.description: ISO 4217 code for credit tiers, when known. `None` for non-credit tiers.

kind.description: Grouping hint for the UI: see [`TierKind`].

resetAt.description:
ISO-8601 reset timestamp. Empty string when unknown — kept as `String`
rather than `Option<String>` for backward compat with older frontends.

spent.description:
Dollar / amount description for budget tiers
(e.g. `"$51.25 / $50.00 spent"`). `None` for non-credit tiers.

UserItemInfo.description: A user-created skill, command, or agent (not from any marketplace).

WeekTrends.description: Collection of all week-over-week trend metrics.

avgReeditRate.description:
Average re-edit rate (weighted average) * 100 for percentage display.
None if no files edited in either period.

avgTokensPerPrompt.description:
Average tokens per prompt (weighted average).
None if no prompts in either period.

WeekTrendsResponse.description: Legacy trends response wrapper with additive metadata.
